context: A straightforward and essential question to ask for directions to an eating establishment.

front: Uma mesa para dois, por favor.
pronunciation: OO-mah MEH-zah PAH-rah doys, poor fah-VOHR.
context: The standard, polite phrase to use when arriving at a restaurant with a companion.
The physical or digital list of food and drink options available at the restaurant.
Polite ways to call over your server. 'Garçom' is for men; 'Garçonete' is for women.
A friendly way to ask your server for their top suggestions or popular dishes.

front: Eu gostaria de pedir um suco.
pronunciation: ew gos-tah-REE-ah jee pee-JEER oong SOO-koo.
back: I would like to order a juice.
context: A polite structure for ordering a beverage. You can swap 'suco' for any drink.

front: Água com gás ou sem gás?
pronunciation: AH-gwah kohng gahs oh sehng gahs?
context: The typical question a server will ask when you order water in Brazil.
Brazilians frequently add ice and a slice of lime to canned sodas or water.
Used by male speakers to indicate dietary preferences. Female speakers say 'vegetariana'.
A helpful question to verify if a dish contains no animal products at all.
Crucial for safety if you have food allergies. Female speakers say 'alérgica'.
Affectionately known as 'PF', this is a classic, affordable Brazilian lunch with rice, beans, and a protein.
The absolute heart and soul of daily Brazilian cuisine, served almost every single day.
Said to others right before eating to wish them a pleasant dining experience.
Used politely by servers when setting down food, or by guests when leaving the table.
A warm, enthusiastic compliment to give your server or the host regarding the food.
Small dishes or snacks served before the arrival of the primary meal course.
The primary, most substantial part of the meal configuration.
The sweet dish eaten at the conclusion of a meal, like brigadeiro or pudim.
It is a massive Brazilian tradition to have a small, sweet coffee at the end of a lunch.
The standard phrase used to signal the waiter that you are finished and ready to pay.
Asks if the standard 10% or 13% tip is already factored into the final bill total.
Extremely common practice among friends or colleagues dining out together in Brazil.
A quick check before finalizing your payment method at the table or register.
Ubiquitous casual eateries in Brazil serving savory snacks, fresh juices, and quick bites.

back: A savory pastry and a chicken croquette
context: Coxinha is the most iconic Brazilian savory snack option found at any lanchonete.
Used when ordering food that you intend to carry out instead of consuming inside.
Informs the counter worker that you will sit down inside the shop to enjoy your food.
The small paper or cloth wipe used for cleaning your hands or mouth during meals.
A basic necessity phrase to ask for a standard drinking glass filled with fresh water.
The primary trio of dining utensils needed for a standard full-course meal setting.
Refers to what is inside a pastry or sandwich, such as cheese, beef, or chicken.
Famous Brazilian restaurant style where servers continuously bring food items to your table.
A polite way to indicate you have eaten enough food. Female speakers say 'satisfeita'.
A casual, natural way to express high satisfaction to the staff before leaving.
